name: TFVoidFractionCorrelations.Hughmark
value:
The Hughmark Void Fraction model equations are solved iteratively to find the void fraction.
It is therefore the most complicated and time-consuming model.
It is a generalization of Bankoff's correlation (1960).  Hughmark found it to work well for
horizontal flow, even though it was developed for vertical upward flow in air-liquid systems.

name: TFRTPDPCorrelations.XuFang2013RTPEvapDP
value:
Xu-Fang Two-Phase Frictional Pressure Drop Correlation for Evaporating Flow in Pipes

Yu Xu, Xiande Fang, 2013, "A new correlation of two-phase frictional pressure drop for evaporating flow in pipes",
Nuclear Engineering and Design 263 (2013) 87– 96

A comprehensive survey of correlations and experimental investigations of two-phase frictional pressure drop is
conducted. The 2622 experimental data points of 15 refrigerants are obtained, with hydraulic diameter from 0.81 to 19.1 mm, mass flux from 25.4 to 1150 kg m 2 s 1, and heat flux from 0.6 to 150 kW m 2. There are 29 existing correlations evaluated against the experimental database. It is found that the best correlation has a mean absolute relative deviation (MARD) of 28.5%. Based on the experimental database, a new correlation is proposed, which has an MARD of 25.2%, improving remarkably the prediction of two-phase frictional pressure drop for pipe evaporating flow, especially for micro-channels.

Includes 15 refrigerants, ammonia, R11, R12, R22, R32, R125, R32/R125 (0.6/0.4,wt), R123, R134a, R407C, R410A, R507, R600a, R290 and CO2.

name: MCRTPHTCCorrelations.PetersKandlikar2007MCEvapRTPHTC
value:
Peters proposed modifications to the use of Kandlikar's Heat Transfer correlation for Evaporaion with deep laminar flows.

Julian V. S. Peters & Satish G. Kandlikar, 2007, "FURTHER EVALUATION OF A FLOW BOILING CORRELATION FOR MICROCHANNELS AND MINICHANNELS", Proceedings of the Fifth International Conference on Nanochannels, Microchannels and Minichannels, CNMM2007-30027, June 18-20, 2007, Puebla, Mexico.

For liquid-only Reynolds numbers between 100 and 410, only the NBD correlation applies. For very low Reynolds numbers, generally under 100, the convective boiling term is removed from the NBD correlation.


Also refer to 
SATISH G. KANDLIKAR & PRABHU BALASUBRAMANIAN, 2004, "An Extension of the Flow Boiling Correlation to Transition, Laminar, and Deep Laminar Flows in Minichannels and Microchannels", Heat Transfer Engineering, 25(3):86–93, 2004

name: TFRTPTHCCorrelations.GrecoVanoliTFRTPHTC
value:
Greco and Vanoli proposed modifications to the use of Kandlikar's Heat Transfer correlation for Evaporaion with deep laminar flows.

Greco a, G.P. Vanoli b, 2005, "Flow boiling heat transfer with HFC mixtures in a smooth horizontal tube. Part II: Assessment of predictive methods", Experimental Thermal and Fluid Science 29 (2005) 199–208

R410A and R404A have been experimentally investigated in a smooth horizontal tube made of stainless steel with an inner diameter of 6 mm and a length of 6 m, uniformly heated by the Joule effect. The evaporation pressures has been varied within the range from 3 to 12 bar, the refrigerant mass flux within the range from 290 to 1100 kg/m2 s and the heat flux within the range from 11 to 39 kW/m2. A modification of the Kandlikar correlation has been proposed to predict the local heat transfer coefficients obtained with the test facility. A correction factor that enhance the influence of the nucleate boiling term has been introduced to take into account the influence of the reduced pressure on the heat transfer coefficients.

Also refer to 
SATISH G. KANDLIKAR & PRABHU BALASUBRAMANIAN, 2004, "An Extension of the Flow Boiling Correlation to Transition, Laminar, and Deep Laminar Flows in Minichannels and Microchannels", Heat Transfer Engineering, 25(3):86–93, 2004
